2605.18692.title: Democratizing Large-Scale Re-Optimization with LLM-Guided Model Patches
2605.18692.paper_summary: Optimization models developed by operations research (OR) experts are often deployed as decision-support systems in industrial settings. However, real-world environments are dynamic, with evolving business rules, previously overlooked constraints, and unforeseen perturbations. In such contexts, end users must rapidly re-optimize models to recover feasible and implementable solutions. This paper introduces an agentic re-optimization framework in which a large language model (LLM) acts as an OR expert, dynamically supporting end users through natural-language interaction. The LLM translates user prompts into structured updates of the underlying optimization model, selects suitable re-optimization techniques from an optimization toolbox, and solves the resulting instance to return implementable solutions. The toolbox leverages primal information, including historical solutions, valid inequalities, solver configurations, and metaheuristics, to accelerate re-optimization while preserving solution quality. The proposed framework enables interactive and continuous adaptation of deployed optimization models, reducing dependence on OR experts and improving the sustainability of decision-support systems. Extensive experiments on two complementary large-scale real-world case studies demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of the proposed framework. The first considers online supply chain re-optimization, where solutions must be generated rapidly while remaining close to the deployed plan, whereas the second focuses on offline university exam scheduling, where solution quality is prioritized over runtime. Results show that the toolbox-driven architecture significantly improves computational efficiency through primal-based and solver-aware re-optimization techniques, while the structured patch-based updates improve interpretability and traceability of model modifications.
2605.18692.paper_summary_zh: 優化模型由運籌學（OR）專家開發，通常在工業環境中作為決策支持系統部署。<lb/>然而，現實環境是動態的，商業規則不斷演變，之前被忽視的約束條件以及不可預見的擾動也隨之而來。<lb/>在這樣的情境中，最終用戶必須快速重新優化模型，以恢復可行且可實施的解決方案。<lb/>本文介紹了一種代理式重新優化框架，其中大型語言模型（LLM）作為運籌學專家，通過自然語言互動動態支持最終用戶。<lb/>LLM 將用戶提示轉換為基礎優化模型的結構化更新，從優化工具箱中選擇合適的重新優化技術，並解決生成的實例以返回可實施的解決方案。<lb/>該工具箱利用原始信息，包括歷史解決方案、有效不等式、求解器配置和元啟發式算法，加速重新優化，同時保持解決方案質量。<lb/>所提出的框架使部署的優化模型能夠進行互動和持續的適應，減少對運籌學專家的依賴，並改善決策支持系統的可持續性。<lb/>在兩個互補的大型現實案例研究中進行的廣泛實驗證明了所提出框架的有效性和可擴展性。<lb/>第一個案例考慮了在線供應鏈重新優化，要求快速生成解決方案，同時保持接近已部署計劃，而第二個案例則專注於離線大學考試排程，優先考慮解決方案質量而非運行時間。<lb/>結果顯示，基於工具箱的架構通過基於原始信息和求解器感知的重新優化技術顯著提高了計算效率，而結構化的補丁式更新則改善了模型修改的可解釋性和可追溯性。
2605.18692.author: Tinghan Ye et.al.
2605.18692.authors: Tinghan Ye, Arnaud Deza, Ved Mohan, El Mehdi Er Raqabi, Pascal Van Hentenryck
